name: Poor Man's Jambalaya
recipeIngredient: 6 oz Tasso or other smoked ham, diced
6 oz Andouille sausage or kielbasa, diced
4 cups Basic beef, pork, or chicken stock
recipeInstructions:
1. Thoroughly combine the seasoning mix ingredients in a small bowl and set aside.
2. In a large heavy skillet (preferably cast iron), melt the margarine over high heat.
3. Add the Tasso and Andouille sausage; cook 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
4. Add the onions, celery, bell peppers, seasoning mix, and garlic. Stir well and continue cooking until brown, about 10-12 minutes, stirring occasionally and scraping the pan bottom well.
5. Stir in the rice and cook for an additional 5 minutes, stirring and scraping the pan bottom occasionally.
6. Add the stock, stirring well. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer until the rice is tender but slightly crunchy, about 20 minutes, stirring occasionally toward the end of cooking time.
7. Meanwhile, preheat serving plates in a 250-degree oven.
8. Remove bay leaves and serve immediately. For a main course, portion out 2 cups Jambalaya per heated plate; for an appetizer, serve 1 cup.
